channel.title: Cariboo Signals
channel.copyright: © 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.
channel.itunes:subtitle: Technology and society in rural BC with Riley and Casey
channel.itunes:author: Erich Zirnhelt
channel.itunes:summary:
How do rural communities grow alongside technology? Daily conversations about responsible tech progress in the Cariboo region on the traditional territories of the Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh nations. Riley brings tech systems thinking with rural roots, while Casey focuses on community development. New episodes every day with weekly themes.
Community Accountability: Cariboo Signals scores 18/20 (Exemplary) on the Community Content Compact — Track A (Community-Insider production). Transparency: 4/4 · Source Integrity: 3/3 · Displacement: 2/2 · Consent & Attribution: 3/4 · Benefit Flow: 4/5 · Accountability: 2/2. Produced by Erich Zirnhelt, Horsefly, BC (Secwépemc territory). Corrections and PSA opt-outs: feedback@cariboosignals.ca.
channel.description: How do rural communities grow alongside technology? Daily conversations about responsible tech progress in the Cariboo region on the traditional territories of the Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh nations. Community Content Compact: 18/20 Exemplary (Track A). Self-funded, non-commercial. Producer: Erich Zirnhelt, Horsefly, BC. Corrections: feedback@cariboosignals.ca.

item.title: Working Lands And Industry
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Working Lands & Industry.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] ‘Will miss caring for you’: Clearwater losing 4 doctors, recruitment efforts underway and [TechCrunch] What ClickUp’s mass layoff tells us about the future of work, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can working land operations in the Cariboo maintain long-term viability and succession when land financialization and investor ownership create structural incentives to sell rather than continue farming? Topics include: Land financialization and investor ownership of agricultural operations, Succession planning gaps in working land operations, Alternative ownership models: collective/Indigenous land tenure versus individual ownership, Producer data sovereignty and precision agriculture as competitive differentiation, Financing structures and credit union products designed for agricultural succession rather than liquidation.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Mt. Timothy Ski Society

Sources discussed:

  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] ‘Will miss caring for you’: Clearwat...
  • Marina Temkin (TechCrunch): [TechCrunch] What ClickUp’s mass layoff tells us about the f...
  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] A Cattle Ranch Is Doing What Ivy League Co...

Additional sources provided:

  • Global News: [Global News] Consultations with Sask. producers ‘an opportu...
  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Taseko Mines Ltd. exploring name cha...
  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. fire crew rescues ducklings fro...
  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] No charges approved in 2023 Vancouve...
  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Saanich Police seize $55K worth of f...
  • Simon Spichak (Ricochet): [Ricochet] Inside the black box reshaping Canada’s benefits ...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Low Head Turbine Generates Plenty of Power
  • Jeremy White (WIRED): [WIRED] The Electric Ferrari Luce Is Finally Here
  • https://www.techradar.com/author/leon-poultney (TechRadar): [TechRadar] I’ve driven the smallest Kia EV yet and there’s ...
  • Andrew J. Hawkins (The Verge): [The Verge] Why Nuro thinks being a robotaxi ‘second mover’ ...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] How solo founders use AI to reduce the op...
  • Isaac Phan Nay (The Tyee): [The Tyee] A Very Shiny Debt Trap
  • The Narwhal: [The Narwhal] For many Canadian farmers, selling land can be...
  • https://www.tomsguide.com/author/alice-carroll (Toms Guide): [Toms Guide] When to fertilize tomatoes: the 3-step schedule...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo)
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Working Lands & Industry.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] ‘Will miss caring for you’: Clearwater losing 4 doctors, recruitment efforts underway and [TechCrunch] What ClickUp’s mass layoff tells us about the future of work, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

item.title: Arts, Culture And Digital Storytelling
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Engadget] Spotify is launching verification badges for podcasts to help listeners avoid AI slop and [The Verge] AI video is moving beyond clip slop, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Can technical transparency standards like TRACE effectively govern AI-generated cultural content, or do they require deeper institutional and legislative frameworks to be meaningful? Topics include: TRACE standard components (Transparency, Responsibility, Accountability, Consent, Explainability), Difference between transparency disclosure and authorization consent in cultural contexts, Indigenous data sovereignty and tiered access requirements (Mukurtu systems, OCAP principles), Implementation gaps in standards adoption (auditing infrastructure, platform enforcement), Community-scale accountability versus formal regulatory frameworks.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Central Cariboo Arts & Culture Society

Sources discussed:

  • Lawrence Bonk (Engadget): [Engadget] Spotify is launching verification badges for podc...
  • Janko Roettgers (The Verge): [The Verge] AI video is moving beyond clip slop
  • Ivan Mehta (TechCrunch): [TechCrunch] Spotify adds AI-powered Q&A and briefing genera...
  • Rob Beschizza (Boing Boing): [Boing Boing] 16 bytes of code generate this video and the m...
  • https://newatlas.com/author/david-szondy/ (New Atlas): [New Atlas] Hybrid Texas power plant blends the best of gas ...
  • Shanal Otchere (Resilience.org): [Resilience.org] Seeds Series Volume 2: Building regenerativ...
  • https://www.tomshardware.com/author/etiido-uko (Tom's Hardware): [Tom's Hardware] Huawei claims sanctions-busting breakthroug...

Additional sources provided:

  • NPR Health News: [NPR Health News] Overworked and understaffed: Special ed te...
  • David Nield (ScienceAlert): [ScienceAlert] One Dietary Supplement Was Found to Lower Agg...
  • Lily Hay Newman (WIRED): [WIRED] The AI Era Is Creating a Bug Hunting Arms Race
  • https://www.techradar.com/author/james-barker (TechRadar): [TechRadar] Why fiber is the real secret to scaling intellig...
  • Newsletter: s***@canadaland.com: #1366 Death of a Journalist: Remembering Elaine Dewar
  • Newsletter: s***@canadaland.com: #182 The Canadian Defence Gold Rush
  • Newsletter: s***@canadaland.com: Discover Our Cell Phone Plans | Fizz

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo)
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

item.title: Science, Wonder And The Natural World
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Science, Wonder & the Natural World.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [ScienceDaily] Quantum ghost imaging works using only sunlight in stunning new experiment and [ScienceDaily] Scientists found a hidden Alzheimer’s trigger and shut it down, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can biodiversity monitoring infrastructure be built in the Cariboo to detect ecological changes while protecting Indigenous data sovereignty and ensuring findings actually influence resource decisions? Topics include: Data sovereignty and Indigenous knowledge governance in monitoring systems, iNaturalist and citizen science as infrastructure for biodiversity observation, River dissolved oxygen decline and salmon habitat degradation in the Cariboo, Epistemic authority: what counts as evidence in resource management decisions, Multi-stakeholder convening as a pathway to integrated monitoring and decision-making.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: 100 Mile House Food Bank Society

Sources discussed:

  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Quantum ghost imaging works using only sunlig...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Scientists found a hidden Alzheimer’s trigger...
  • AFP (ScienceAlert): [ScienceAlert] WATCH: A Spacecraft Is Launching to Study How...
  • Jess Cockerill (ScienceAlert): [ScienceAlert] A Beluga Whale Showed a Sign of Intelligence ...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Scientists solve 320-million-year mystery of ...
  • David Nield (ScienceAlert): [ScienceAlert] This Gene Is Linked to Lower Alzheimer's Risk...
  • Michelle Starr (ScienceAlert): [ScienceAlert] Fossil Kept in a Museum For Decades Turns Out...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Scientists discover towering red auroras reac...
  • Jess Cockerill (ScienceAlert): [ScienceAlert] 80% of Earth's Rivers Are Quickly Losing Oxyg...

Additional sources provided:

  • https://refractor.io/author/the-conversation/ (New Atlas): [New Atlas] Historical accidents show just how long 'forever...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Ancient lost ocean may have built Central Asi...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] String theory suddenly emerged from simple ph...
  • https://refractor.io/author/mike-mcrae/ (New Atlas): [New Atlas] The fate of a collapsing star could depend on th...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Rare graves reveal a lost world of Bronze Age...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Hidden earthquake faults beneath Seattle may ...
  • https://www.tomshardware.com/author/etiido-uko (Tom's Hardware): [Tom's Hardware] Laser-driven spintronic memory device switc...
  • Michelle Starr (ScienceAlert): [ScienceAlert] The Great Pyramid Has Survived 4,600 Years. A...
  • Michael Irving (ScienceAlert): [ScienceAlert] Scientists May Finally Know Why T. Rex Had Su...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Antarctic glacier collapses at record speed a...
  • Nautilus: [Nautilus] How to Predict an Earthquake
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Plant believed extinct for 60 years suddenly ...
  • https://refractor.io/team/jay-kakade/ (New Atlas): [New Atlas] Watch: Daredevil fish climb 50-foot waterfall in...

Closing Music: “Breathe” by Bellevue — pop, indie, surfrock. Free music via Jamendo, licensed under Creative Commons.

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno

Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6

item.title: Cariboo Local Affairs
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Cariboo Local Affairs.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake RCMP seek witnesses after assault on Borland Street and [Williams Lake Tribune] Planning underway for National Indigenous Peoples Day celebrations in Williams Lake, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: When Cariboo residents use political tools like petitions, conventions, and community organizing, do these efforts actually produce outcomes or merely create the appearance of participation? Topics include: The 100 Mile House ER petition and rural healthcare staffing challenges, NCLGA conventions as a rural advocacy mechanism versus individual petitions, Barriers to small volunteer community initiatives (Lebourdais Park movie night example), Political endorsements and leadership accountability to rural communities (Doerkson-Milobar endorsement), Digital connectivity as infrastructure for civic self-governance rather than just economic development.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Women's Contact Society

Sources discussed:

  • Laisa Conde (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake RCMP seek witnesses af...
  • rebeccadyok (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Planning underway for National Indig...
  • rebeccadyok (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Early blooms for Williams Lake Harve...
  • Laisa Conde (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake RCMP urge safe off-roa...
  • Laisa Conde (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Nearly 50 firearms seized by RCMP du...
  • Laisa Conde (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake Film Club ends season ...
  • Patrick Davies (100 Mile Free Press): [100 Mile Free Press] South Cariboo locals create petition, ...
  • Laisa Conde (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Confident, strong female skiers invi...
  • none (100 Mile Free Press): [100 Mile Free Press] South Cariboo artist brings love of se...
  • My Cariboo Now: [My Cariboo Now] Movie night in Lebourdais Park in Quesnel i...
  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Cariboo Chilcotin MLA endorses Peter...
  • My Cariboo Now: [My Cariboo Now] Cariboo issues front and center at NCLGA Co...
  • none (100 Mile Free Press): [100 Mile Free Press] GROVE NEWS: Several public meetings co...

Additional sources provided:

  • Laisa Conde (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Cariboo drivers reminded to use caut...
  • Laisa Conde (Quesnel Cariboo Observer): [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Quesnel River Rush battles throug...
  • Paul Rodgers (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Kimberley remembers: 20 years since ...
  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] ‘Tragic accident’: Woman presumed dr...
  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Site C Dam renamed for former premie...
  • Jennifer Smith (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Vernon crews helping in Kamloops riv...
  • Evert Lindquist (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Man earns conditional discharge for ...
  • none (Quesnel Cariboo Observer): [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] 7 non-profits come together to fo...
  • My Cariboo Now: [My Cariboo Now] Man arrested in Walmart Parking lot in Ques...

Closing Music: “Breathe” by Bellevue — pop, indie, surfrock. Free music via Jamendo, licensed under Creative Commons.

Credits

Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6

item.title: Wild Spaces And Outdoor Life
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [WIRED] California’s Wildfire Season Is Already Overactive and [BC Gov News] Inviting public to comment on Sea to Sky land use objectives, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Should post-fire landscapes be managed back to their prior state, or monitored as dynamic systems in transition, and what role should technology play in that process? Topics include: Post-fire landscape recovery and ecological monitoring strategies, Technology integration for wildlife tracking and public safety (GPS collars, eDNA, trail apps), Data governance, liability, and sustainability of public-facing systems, First Nations data sovereignty and the Tŝilhqot'in Yalhiyalh program, Regional land use planning processes and recreational pressure on conservation areas.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: First Journey Trails & Indigenous Youth Mountain Bike Program

Sources discussed:

  • Brian Kahn (WIRED): [WIRED] California’s Wildfire Season Is Already Overactive
  • BC Gov News: [BC Gov News] Inviting public to comment on Sea to Sky land ...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Helicopters douse flames threatening hi...
  • Global News: [Global News] Nova Scotia’s new firefighting aircraft squelc...
  • Tyler Harper (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. sailor selected for Antarctica ...
  • My East Kootenay Now: [My East Kootenay Now] Grizzly and cubs spotted near Westsid...
  • Lachlan Labere (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Province readies wildfire damaged Sh...

Additional sources provided:

item.itunes:summary:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [WIRED] California’s Wildfire Season Is Already Overactive and [BC Gov News] Inviting public to comment on Sea to Sky land use objectives, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Should post-fire landscapes be managed back to their prior state, or monitored as dynamic systems in transition, and what role should technology play in that process? Topics include: Post-fire landscape recovery and ecological monitoring strategies, Technology integration for wildlife tracking and public safety (GPS collars, eDNA, trail apps), Data governance, liability, and sustainability of public-facing systems, First Nations data sovereignty and the Tŝilhqot'in Yalhiyalh program, Regional land use planning processes and recreational pressure on conservation areas.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: First Journey Trails & Indigenous Youth Mountain Bike Program

Sources discussed:

  • Brian Kahn (WIRED): [WIRED] California’s Wildfire Season Is Already Overactive
  • BC Gov News: [BC Gov News] Inviting public to comment on Sea to Sky land ...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Helicopters douse flames threatening hi...
  • Global News: [Global News] Nova Scotia’s new firefighting aircraft squelc...

  • My East Kootenay Now: [My East Kootenay Now] Grizzly and cubs spotted near Westsid...

  • Tim Fernholz (TechCrunch): [TechCrunch] Convective Capital raises an $85 million fund t...

item.title: Indigenous Lands And Innovation
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Indigenous Lands & Innovation.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down Technology shouldn't sacrifice the integrity of knowledge and Seattle University School of Law Hosts Conference on Indigenous Perspectives on AI, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: When technology enables discovery of concealed evidence (like residential school graves), how should communities balance the capacity to surface knowledge with governance frameworks that protect how that knowledge is used and disclosed? Topics include: Data sovereignty and community consent in residential school investigations, Technology capabilities (GPR, LiDAR) vs. governance frameworks for knowledge use, Federal funding architecture and its role as both enabler and gatekeeper, Academic research partnerships and data storage/publication control, AI risk to Indigenous knowledge in publicly accessible datasets.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Cariboo Chilcotin Aboriginal Training Employment Centre

Sources discussed:

  • Newsletter: n***@animikii.com: Technology shouldn't sacrifice the integrity of knowledge
  • Newsletter: n***@animikii.com: Seattle University School of Law Hosts Conference on Indigen...
  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Heiltsuk Nation settles Coquitlam Ca...
  • Dell Cameron (WIRED): [WIRED] A Bipartisan Amendment Would End Police License Plat...
  • Jason Weisberger (Boing Boing): [Boing Boing] AI's giant rural job machine mostly appears to...
  • Paresh Dave (WIRED): [WIRED] SpaceX Is Spending $2.8 Billion to Buy Gas Turbines ...
  • Newsletter: s***@canadaland.com: #1362 The Truth About Residential School Graves

Additional sources provided:

  • CNET: [CNET] Scientists Warn AI Slop Is Wreaking Havoc in the Rese...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] Civil engineering researchers are teachin...
  • Tim De Chant (TechCrunch): [TechCrunch] Global EV market goes K-shaped as the US gets l...
  • The Maple: [The Maple] Find Out If Your Provincial Rep Is A Landlord
  • The Maple: [The Maple] Ontario, Find Out If Your MPP Is A Landlord
  • The Maple: [The Maple] British Columbia, Find Out If Your MLA Is A Land...
  • Newsletter: s***@canadaland.com: #181 Avi Lewis: the Canadaland Interview
  • Newsletter: s***@canadaland.com: Discover Our Cell Phone Plans | Fizz

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo)
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Indigenous Lands & Innovation.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down Technology shouldn't sacrifice the integrity of knowledge and Seattle University School of Law Hosts Conference on Indigenous Perspectives on AI, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: When technology enables discovery of concealed evidence (like residential school graves), how should communities balance the capacity to surface knowledge with governance frameworks that protect how that knowledge is used and disclosed? Topics include: Data sovereignty and community consent in residential school investigations, Technology capabilities (GPR, LiDAR) vs. governance frameworks for knowledge use, Federal funding architecture and its role as both enabler and gatekeeper, Academic research partnerships and data storage/publication control, AI risk to Indigenous knowledge in publicly accessible datasets.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

item.title: Gear, Gadgets And Practical Tech
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Gear, Gadgets & Practical Tech.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Hackaday] Using a Nintendo Switch to Speed Up a 3D Printer and [WIRED] Oto Smart Sprinkler Review (2026): Solar-Powered and Simple to Use, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: In geographically isolated rural regions like the Cariboo, what combination of technology, maintenance practices, and community infrastructure actually enables sustainable self-sufficiency and emergency preparedness? Topics include: Emergency preparedness technology: single-use vs. maintained rechargeable devices and their real-world reliability in rural heat conditions, 3D printing, ESP32 microcontrollers, and open-source fabrication as accessibility tools with real skill and infrastructure barriers, Proprietary vs. open-source firmware lock-in (Bambu Lab case study) and its disproportionate impact on geographically isolated small operators, Institutional sustainability of technical skill-building programs and knowledge transfer in communities with high population turnover, Data sovereignty and community control over fabrication tools in Indigenous and rural contexts.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Boys and Girls Club of Williams Lake

Sources discussed:

  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Using a Nintendo Switch to Speed Up a 3D Printer
  • Christopher Null (WIRED): [WIRED] Oto Smart Sprinkler Review (2026): Solar-Powered and...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Fixing a Cotton Candy Vending Machine
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] A Status Screen For Bambu Labs Printers
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Teardown: ChargeTab Emergency Phone Charger
  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] 4 3D printer upgrades you can build with a $5 ...
  • https://www.tomshardware.com/author/denise-bertacchi (Tom's Hardware): [Tom's Hardware] Louis Rossmann taunts Bambu Lab by hosting ...

Additional sources provided:

  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Turning a Junk Laptop Screen Into a Portable Moni...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] preFlight Slicer Brings Added Part Strength Featu...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Y-zipper: 3D Printing Flexible–Rigid Transition M...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] 21st Century Punch Cards are 3D Printed and Read ...
  • https://www.techradar.com/author/steve-clark (TechRadar): [TechRadar] Bluetti says home solar power is now ‘plug-and-p...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo)
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt

Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Gear, Gadgets & Practical Tech.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Hackaday] Using a Nintendo Switch to Speed Up a 3D Printer and [WIRED] Oto Smart Sprinkler Review (2026): Solar-Powered and Simple to Use, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: In geographically isolated rural regions like the Cariboo, what combination of technology, maintenance practices, and community infrastructure actually enables sustainable self-sufficiency and emergency preparedness? Topics include: Emergency preparedness technology: single-use vs. maintained rechargeable devices and their real-world reliability in rural heat conditions, 3D printing, ESP32 microcontrollers, and open-source fabrication as accessibility tools with real skill and infrastructure barriers, Proprietary vs. open-source firmware lock-in (Bambu Lab case study) and its disproportionate impact on geographically isolated small operators, Institutional sustainability of technical skill-building programs and knowledge transfer in communities with high population turnover, Data sovereignty and community control over fabrication tools in Indigenous and rural contexts.

item.title: Working Lands And Industry
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Working Lands & Industry.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [ScienceDaily] AI reveals the invisible magnetic chaos wasting energy inside electric motors and [Tom's Hardware] Texas county passes data center ban for rural areas for a year, move comes in wake of AI data centers moving to remote areas to skirt regulations — state senator says counties cannot legally impose these bans, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Does the Cariboo have the institutional capacity to adopt agricultural innovations and manage competing land uses, or is the regional governance infrastructure too thin to implement solutions regardless of technological readiness? Topics include: Perennial grain economics and the yield-penalty transition problem in Cariboo agriculture, Agricultural financing gaps and why lender structures prevent innovation adoption, Regional research capacity deficits and the mismatch between global research agendas and local agricultural needs, Data centre land use competition with agriculture and grid reliability impacts, Indigenous-led land stewardship as an institutional capacity model, The timing mismatch between technological readiness, institution-building, and external land use pressures.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Cariboo Chilcotin Coast Tourism Association

Sources discussed:

  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] AI reveals the invisible magnetic chaos wasti...
  • Tom's Hardware: [Tom's Hardware] Texas county passes data center ban for rur...
  • Quartz: [Quartz] What 'cascading outages' near AI data centers mean ...
  • APTN News: [APTN News] Baffinland Iron Mines seeking creditor protectio...

Additional sources provided:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Environmental advocates call pipelin...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Wild world record broken by barefoot...
  • NYT Business: [NYT Business] A Start-Up Aiming to Make Geothermal Energy M...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Scientists just unlocked a cheaper way to mak...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] RS-485 Sprinkler Control: Scaling Irrigation Acro...
  • Neowin: [Neowin] Solar power could finally be viable with this inven...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Forget electrons, this breakthrough uses ligh...
  • Tom's Hardware: [Tom's Hardware] SoftBank to manufacture its own batteries w...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] This US start-up wants to pay you to host a data...
  • InsideEVs: [InsideEVs] Congress Wants You To Pay $130 A Year Just To Dr...
  • Krebs on Security: [Krebs on Security] CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on G...
  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] My Classmate, ChatGPT
  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] Genetic discovery could change how rice is grown...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] US data centers use enough electricity to power ...
  • Tom's Hardware: [Tom's Hardware] OpenClaw creator burned through $1.3 millio...

Credits

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Does the Cariboo have the institutional capacity to adopt agricultural innovations and manage competing land uses, or is the regional governance infrastructure too thin to implement solutions regardless of technological readiness? item.title: Arts, Culture And Digital Storytelling
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [TechCrunch] Wirestock raises $23M to supply creative multi-modal data to AI labs and [Open Culture] When the Nobel Prize Committee Rejected The Lord of the Rings: Tolkien “Has Not Measured Up to Storytelling of the Highest Quality” (1961), plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: When, if ever, is AI-generated cultural content justified, and what governance structures and guardrails should protect communities from extraction while enabling legitimate cultural participation? Topics include: NPR study on arts engagement and biological aging at cellular level, AI content flooding discoverability landscapes and what it displaces versus what it doesn't, Indigenous sovereignty and community control over cultural production tools and governance, Project-based funding gaps and the failure of pilots to become sustainable infrastructure, Extraction models in tech (Wirestock, IP-as-infrastructure) versus community-serving design.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Cariboo Chilcotin Partners for Literacy

Sources discussed:

  • TechCrunch: [TechCrunch] Wirestock raises $23M to supply creative multi-...
  • Open Culture: [Open Culture] When the Nobel Prize Committee Rejected The L...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Mixapps: The Mixtape of The Internet Age
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] We’re Only Human. And That’ll Do
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] MrBeast revealed a new membership program...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Lost 1,200-year-old manuscript contains the f...
  • IndigiNews: [IndigiNews] Introducing our 2026 ReFocus photojournalism fe...
  • NPR Health News: [NPR Health News] Engaging with the arts can slow biological...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] “I cannot divorce the two”: How Star Wars is ble...
  • IndigiNews: [IndigiNews] A new GyaaG̲ang (totem pole) is raised in Haida...

Additional sources provided:

  • Neowin: [Neowin] Spotify will soon support Apple's technology to ope...
  • The Marginalian: [The Marginalian] How to Keep Criticism from Sinking Your So...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] Paramount+ is exploring adding podcasts, ...
  • The Verge: [The Verge] YouTube is courting creators — and sponsors — wi...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Heartbreak in Dawson City after hist...
  • Resilience.org: [Resilience.org] In the Rising Tide, Episode 5. Raviraj Shet...
  • Lifehacker: [Lifehacker] 10 Shows Like 'Paradise' You Should Watch Next
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] As Election Looms, Band Halts Chief’s Credit Card...
  • Toms Guide: [Toms Guide] What being a merch girl for Amy Winehouse and J...
  • The Atlantic Culture: [The Atlantic Culture] When Bots Write Comedy, the Joke’s on...
  • Engadget: [Engadget] Apple acquired another company that could help bu...
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] Join Us for Reality Check LIVE on May 21

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno

Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6

  • Engadget: [Engadget] Apple acquired another company that could help bu...
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] Join Us for Reality Check LIVE on May 21

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo)
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

item.title: Science, Wonder And The Natural World
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Science, Wonder & the Natural World.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [ScienceDaily] Stunning 150-million-year-old stegosaur skull rewrites dinosaur evolution and [ScienceDaily] Paleontology rocked by discovery of organic molecules in 66-million-year-old dinosaur bones, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can the Cariboo region develop locally-controlled, long-term scientific monitoring infrastructure using accessible methodologies without reproducing extractive research models? Topics include: Environmental DNA methodology for monitoring aquatic species without capture or disturbance, Indigenous data sovereignty and governance requirements for research conducted on traditional territories, Acoustic ecology and plant responses to environmental sound in forest regeneration contexts, Old-growth forests as scientific archives containing climate and cosmic event records, Community foundations and endowment models as sustainable funding vehicles for long-term ecological monitoring.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Quesnel Community Foundation

Sources discussed:

  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Stunning 150-million-year-old stegosaur skull...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Paleontology rocked by discovery of organic m...
  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] JWST discovers a galaxy that doesn’t spin in the...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Scientists discover the Southern Ocean is “sw...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] The Earliest Known Dentistry Wasn't Done By O...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Crickets Respond to Injuries in a Way That Su...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] JWST Reveals a Hidden Structure in The Heart ...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] First-ever direct image of the cosmic web rev...
  • Nautilus: [Nautilus] Perseverance Snaps a Selfie on Mars
  • Nautilus: [Nautilus] Newly Discovered Asteroid to Make Close Pass by E...
  • WIRED: [WIRED] Asteroid 2026 JH2 Is About to Fly Right Past Earth—R...

Additional sources provided:

  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] After 100 years, scientists finally uncover h...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Scientists reversed memory loss by recharging...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Who are the Japanese? Huge DNA discovery rewr...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Surprise X-Ray Discovery May Explain Strange ...
  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] Neanderthal dentists used stone drills to fix an...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] First Signatures of a Future Tectonic Split A...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Deadly “red sky” solar storm from 800 years a...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] NASA’s Hubble reveals a giant chaotic planet...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Scientists put a tiny lump of metal in two pl...
  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] Record-breaking volcanic eruption somehow cleane...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Stardust Trapped in Antarctic Ice Reveals Ear...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Quantum breakthrough could revolutionize tele...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Giant squid discovery uncovers a hidden deep-...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Earth is flying through ancient supernova deb...
  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] Plants may literally hear the sound of approachi...

Closing Music: “Behind The Walls” by Non Camera — pop, rock, indie. Free music via Jamendo, licensed under Creative Commons.

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo)
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.
item.itunes:summary:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Science, Wonder & the Natural World.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [ScienceDaily] Stunning 150-million-year-old stegosaur skull rewrites dinosaur evolution and [ScienceDaily] Paleontology rocked by discovery of organic molecules in 66-million-year-old dinosaur bones, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can the Cariboo region develop locally-controlled, long-term scientific monitoring infrastructure using accessible methodologies without reproducing extractive research models? Topics include: Environmental DNA methodology for monitoring aquatic species without capture or disturbance, Indigenous data sovereignty and governance requirements for research conducted on traditional territories, Acoustic ecology and plant responses to environmental sound in forest regeneration contexts, Old-growth forests as scientific archives containing climate and cosmic event records, Community foundations and endowment models as sustainable funding vehicles for long-term ecological monitoring.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Quesnel Community Foundation
